methodology: Articles sourced from Virginia-specific RSS feeds and Google News RSS, filtered for AI/technology/workforce relevance, then assessed by Claude against the scenario framework. Each article is tagged with the region it most affects and the scenario it signals toward.

signals.title: New partnership between VCU and AI Ready RVA aims to boost AI education, innovation and community impact - VCU News
signals.signal_reading: VCU's partnership with AI Ready RVA signals deliberate distribution of AI fluency through community education and innovation initiatives beyond elite tech hubs, creating peer learning infrastructure that could spread deep capability across Richmond's diverse workforce.

signals.title: EPA grant backs community-driven workforce training in Southwest Virginia - Virginia Tech News
signals.signal_reading: EPA-funded community-driven workforce training in Southwest Virginia represents grassroots capability building in an economically transitioning region, suggesting distributed skill development rather than platform dependency or metro concentration.

signals.title: NoVA School Systems Grapple with AI’s Role in the Classroom - Northern Virginia Magazine
signals.signal_reading: NoVA schools grappling with AI in classrooms indicates early adoption through consumer platforms rather than deep technical fluency, pointing toward shallow-but-distributed use cases unless paired with substantive CS education investments.

signals.title: AI pressure grows on Richmond's young workforce - MSN
signals.signal_reading: Pressure on Richmond's young workforce suggests AI-driven job displacement anxieties without corresponding skill-building infrastructure, signaling potential shallow adoption and workforce disruption rather than empowerment or distributed capability.

signals.title: Hampton Roads multiple listing service introduces AI tool to personalize home searches - The Virginian-Pilot
signals.signal_reading: Hampton Roads MLS deploying turnkey AI personalization tools exemplifies platform-delivered capability enabling broad but shallow adoption across the real estate sector without requiring deep technical expertise from end users.
